user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit claims total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, seeking refund with interest and costs.

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with District Court based on pecuniary jurisdiction
Suit is filed within limitation period

Sub-rule 5 of r 15 enabled the court to direct the parties to furnish better statements of the nature of the claim or 
defence, or further and better particulars of any matter stated in any pleading. This rule is perhaps omitted on the 
ground that it is unnecessary in view of the provisions for serving the interrogatories and the provisions relating to 
discovery and inspection. This omission is being effected in consistency with the other changes made in the Code. 
The Law Commission in its 163rd report was of the opinion that it was safely omitted.
Insertion of sub-r (4) in r 15 provides that the person verifying the pleading shall also furnish an affidavit in support 
of his pleadings. This insertion is in consonance with the amended s 26 which requires that in every plaint, fact shall 
be proved by affidavit. By this sub-r (4) the requirement of affidavit is not limited only to plaint but extends to written 
statement as well. The Law Commission had strongly recommended this amendment in its present format. It

S. 26. Institution of suits.
91[(1)]Every suit shall be instituted by the presentation of a plaint or in such other manner as may be 
prescribed.
[(2) In every plaint, facts shall be proved by affidavit].
The Amending Act of 1999 has inserted a new sub-s (2) in s 26. The old s 26 has been re-numbered as sub-s 
(1). The Amending Act comes into effect from 1 July 2002. It shall not apply to or affect any suit pending 
immediately before the commencement of amending provisions and every such suit shall be tried as if the 
amending provision had not come into force.
The amended s 26 requires that in every plaint, facts shall be proved by an affidavit. The requirement of 
affidavit is not limited only to plaint but extends to the written statement as well, as it appears from the insertion 
of sub-r (3) of r 15 of O 6. The said rule provides that person verifying the pleading shall also furnish an affidavit

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- with Interest and Costs against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

Suit No. __________ of 20__

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged ___, Occupation: ___, Address: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} (hereinafter referred to as the 'Plaintiff')
Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged ____, Occupation: ___, Address: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} (hereinafter referred to as the 'Defendant')

A suit for Recovery of Money under Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

The Plaintiff states as follows:

Facts and cause of action

1. The Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) as an advance to the Defendant for a business transaction. The said transaction failed solely due to the Defendant's default, resulting in total failure of consideration. The Plaintiff seeks refund of the aforesaid amount along with costs and interest.

2. The amount claimed is Rs. 20,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Lakh). Date of payment: {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}. The Plaintiff pleads total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 (for breach of contract by non-fulfillment of the consideration).

3. The present cause of action arises from the date of payment and continues to date, as the Defendant has not refunded the amount despite requests and due demand. The cause of action is of a continuing nature until realization of the refund, together with interest and costs. The accrual date is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}.

4. For the avoidance of doubt, the Plaintiff pleads that the Defendant's default and the consequent total failure of consideration render the advance payment repayable on demand, and the Plaintiff seeks restitution and return of the amount paid as advance, with interest and costs.

5. Jurisdiction and venue: This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain this suit under its pecuniary jurisdiction in the District Court at {{PLACE}} within {{STATE}}. The Plaintiff asserts that the suit is properly valued for court fees and jurisdiction at Rs. 20,00,000. The exact court fee payable is to be calculated as per the applicable Court Fee Rules and is presently stated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}. Court fee details may be verified at the time of filing.

6. Notice and demand: The Plaintiff has complied with the procedural prerequisites to the extent possible and provided notice/demand details as {{NOTICE_DETAILS}}. If any further notice is required, it may be treated as provided in law and the Court may direct as necessary.

7. The Plaintiff claims relief as hereinafter stated, with costs and interest as per law and as directed by this Hon'ble Court.

Prayer

A. Decree for refund of Rs. 20,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) along with interest on the whole sum at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable from the date of accrual {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till payment, and up to the date of realization, and costs of the suit.
B. Any other relief that this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Verification

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby solemnly verify and declare that the contents of the above plaint are true to my knowledge and belief and that the facts stated in paras 1 to 7 are true and correct and no part thereof is false. Glancing at the facts, I verify on oath that the cause of action arose on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} and is continuing in nature until the relief sought is granted.

Place: {{PLACE}}, Date: {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}

_________________________
Plaintiff/Authorized Representative
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (Address: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}})

Annexures: Annexure A – Copy of payment receipt or evidence of payment (if available); Annexure B – Any communications with the Defendant (if available). The specific annexures to be attached will be enumerated and labeled at the time of filing.

List of Documents / Enclosures
1. Annexure A – Copy of payment receipt (if available).
2. Annexure B – Correspondence with Defendant (if any).
3. Any other documents as may be relied upon at the time of hearing, appropriately labeled and indexed.

Notes for filing
- Placeholder values shall be replaced with actual details at the time of filing. The court fee payable shall be calculated per the applicable Court Fee Rules and paid in full at the time of filing; the final amount may be adjusted after admission, if required. The Plaintiff shall verify the correctness of the valuation for court fee purposes.

Placeholders used: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (Plaintiff's name not provided); {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (Defendant's name not provided); {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} (Plaintiff's address not provided); {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} (Defendant's address not provided); {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (Date of payment not provided); {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} (Accrual date not provided); {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (Court fee amount not available — to be verified); {{NOTICE_DETAILS}} (Notice details not provided); {{STATE}} (State not provided); {{PLACE}} (Place not provided); {{VERIFICATION_DATE}} (Date of verification not provided).

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- with Interest and Costs against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

Suit No. __________ of 20__

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged ___ years,
Occupation: ___,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
…Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged ___ years,
Occupation: ___,
Residing/Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
…Defendant

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY (REFUND OF ADVANCE) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

The Plaintiff states as follows:

1. Parties
1.1 The Plaintiff is an adult and is competent to institute the present suit.
1.2 The Defendant is an adult and is liable to be sued before this Hon’ble Court.

2. Facts giving rise to the claim
2.1 On {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) to the Defendant as an advance in connection with a proposed business transaction, particulars whereof are as follows: {{TRANSACTION_DETAILS}}.

2.2 The said transaction failed and the consideration for which the said advance was paid failed on {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}} due to the Defendant’s acts/omissions, particulars whereof are: {{DEFENDANT_DEFAULT_PARTICULARS}}.

2.3 Upon such failure of consideration, the Defendant became bound to refund/restitute the amount received from the Plaintiff under the principle of restitution/total failure of consideration, including as contemplated under {{SECTION_65_INDIAN_CONTRACT_ACT_1872}} (to be verified prior to filing).

2.4 Despite demands made by the Plaintiff on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} through {{MODE_OF_DEMAND}} and thereafter including on {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}}, the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/-.

3. Cause of action and limitation
3.1 The cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}}, being the date on which the consideration failed and the Plaintiff’s right to claim refund/restitution accrued.

3.2 The cause of action further/again arose on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and on each subsequent refusal/failure by the Defendant to refund, including on {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}}.

3.3 The Plaintiff submits that the suit is within limitation. The Plaintiff relies on Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 ("For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails" – limitation of three years from "the date of the failure").

4. Jurisdiction
4.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within its jurisdiction, inter alia, at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}} and/or the Defendant resides/carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

4.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit is Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) excluding future interest and costs, and is within the pecuniary limits of this Hon’ble Court.

5. Valuation and court fee
5.1 For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fees, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only).

5.2 The court fee payable is ad valorem on the suit claim as per the applicable State Court Fees law/rules. Since the jurisdiction-specific rate is not provided in the record, the Plaintiff states the court fee as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} subject to verification and payment of correct court fee at the time of filing.

6. No other proceedings
6.1 The Plaintiff states that no previous suit between the parties for the same cause of action and same relief has been filed, except: {{PRIOR_PROCEEDINGS_IF_ANY}}.

7. Reliefs claimed (Prayer)
The Plaintiff therefore prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

A. Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only);

B. Award interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}} (date of failure of consideration/accrual) till the date of filing of the suit, and further interest pendente lite and future till realization, as this Hon’ble Court deems fit;

C. Award costs of the suit; and

D. Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 5 are true to my personal knowledge and those of paragraphs 6 to 7 are based on legal advice and information believed to be true. Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}.


_________________________
Plaintiff
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

Through Counsel:
{{COUNSEL_NAME}}


LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ENCLOSURES
1. Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (receipt/bank statement/transfer proof) – Annexure A.
2. Documents/communications evidencing the proposed transaction and its terms – Annexure B.
3. Demand communication(s) dated {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}} along with proof of dispatch/delivery – Annexure C.
4. Any other documents relied upon – Annexure D (to be specified).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The said transaction failed solely due to the Defendant's default”
unsupported_statements.reason: No underlying particulars of the transaction, contractual obligations, or specific acts/omissions constituting “default” are provided in the draft beyond a conclusory assertion; needs supporting facts (what was agreed, what was to be performed, what was not performed, and when).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The present cause of action arises from the date of payment and continues to date, as the Defendant has not refunded the amount despite requests and due demand.”
unsupported_statements.reason: The draft does not provide dates/modes of requests/demand or the legally relevant accrual event (date of failure of consideration / date refund became due).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The Plaintiff has complied with the procedural prerequisites to the extent possible and provided notice/demand details as {{NOTICE_DETAILS}}. If any further notice is required, it may be treated as provided in law…”
unsupported_statements.reason: No facts are pleaded showing any actual notice/demand; additionally, statutory notice (if any) cannot be deemed served by pleading.

final_artifacts.title: Plaint under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- with Interest and Costs against {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}
final_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

Suit No. __________ of 20__

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged ___ years,
Occupation: ___,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
…Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged ___ years,
Occupation: ___,
Residing/Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
…Defendant

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY (REFUND OF ADVANCE) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

The Plaintiff states as follows:

1. Parties
1.1 The Plaintiff is an adult and is competent to institute the present suit.
1.2 The Defendant is an adult and is liable to be sued before this Hon’ble Court.

2. Facts giving rise to the claim
2.1 On {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) to the Defendant as an advance in connection with a proposed business transaction, particulars whereof are as follows: {{TRANSACTION_DETAILS}}.

2.2 The said transaction failed and the consideration for which the said advance was paid failed on {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}} due to the Defendant’s acts/omissions, particulars whereof are: {{DEFENDANT_DEFAULT_PARTICULARS}}.

2.3 Upon such failure of consideration, the Defendant became bound to refund/restitute the amount received from the Plaintiff under the principle of restitution/total failure of consideration, including as contemplated under {{SECTION_65_INDIAN_CONTRACT_ACT_1872}} (to be verified prior to filing).

2.4 Despite demands made by the Plaintiff on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} through {{MODE_OF_DEMAND}} and thereafter including on {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}}, the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/-.

3. Cause of action and limitation
3.1 The cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}}, being the date on which the consideration failed and the Plaintiff’s right to claim refund/restitution accrued.

3.2 The cause of action further/again arose on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and on each subsequent refusal/failure by the Defendant to refund, including on {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}}.

3.3 The Plaintiff submits that the suit is within limitation. The Plaintiff relies on Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 ("For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails" – limitation of three years from "the date of the failure").

4. Jurisdiction
4.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the cause of action arose within its jurisdiction, inter alia, at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}} and/or the Defendant resides/carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

4.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit is Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) excluding future interest and costs, and is within the pecuniary limits of this Hon’ble Court.

5. Valuation and court fee
5.1 For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fees, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only).

5.2 The court fee payable is ad valorem on the suit claim as per the applicable State Court Fees law/rules. Since the jurisdiction-specific rate is not provided in the record, the Plaintiff states the court fee as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} subject to verification and payment of correct court fee at the time of filing.

6. No other proceedings
6.1 The Plaintiff states that no previous suit between the parties for the same cause of action and same relief has been filed, except: {{PRIOR_PROCEEDINGS_IF_ANY}}.

7. Reliefs claimed (Prayer)
The Plaintiff therefore prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

A. Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only);

B. Award interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{DATE_OF_FAILURE_OF_CONSIDERATION}} (date of failure of consideration/accrual) till the date of filing of the suit, and further interest pendente lite and future till realization, as this Hon’ble Court deems fit;

C. Award costs of the suit; and

D. Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 5 are true to my personal knowledge and those of paragraphs 6 to 7 are based on legal advice and information believed to be true. Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}.


_________________________
Plaintiff
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

Through Counsel:
{{COUNSEL_NAME}}


LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ENCLOSURES
1. Proof of payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (receipt/bank statement/transfer proof) – Annexure A.
2. Documents/communications evidencing the proposed transaction and its terms – Annexure B.
3. Demand communication(s) dated {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}} and {{DATE_OF_LAST_DEMAND}} along with proof of dispatch/delivery – Annexure C.
4. Any other documents relied upon – Annexure D (to be specified).

placeholders_used.reason: The draft cited Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 but no RAG chunk provided the provision text/confirmation; must be verified before filing
Underlying business transaction particulars not provided
Date when consideration failed (accrual date per limitation) not provided
Specific acts/omissions constituting default not provided
Jurisdiction-specific court fee rate not provided in COURT_FEE_CONTEXT; must be computed as per applicable State Court Fees law
No information provided regarding prior proceedings; placeholder retained to avoid incorrect assertion
Interest rate sought not specified; to be decided as per contract/practice and pleaded
